description:
Collapse the three independent spots that derive the delivery code-worktree path + 'work/<id>' branch into a single helper, so they cannot drift when §11 claimant-keying lands.

CONTEXT. Frozen architecture (docs/architecture.md, bl-cac0 coherence pass bl-7d46) §11 now states verbatim: 'worktree_path(binding, id [, claimant]) = .../<key>/ ; branch = work/<key>  # <key> = <id>, or <id>-<claimant>'. Rationale (verbatim): 'Keying on claimant as well as id ... disambiguates a drop-and-reclaim by a different actor and naming forge branches by owner.' This is a SPEC REVISION not yet implemented — all code today keys on <id> only, so it is currently consistent.

CURRENT STATE. The (worktree_path, 'work/{id}' branch) pair is derived in THREE places that must move together when claimant-keying is implemented:
  1. src/bin/bl-delivery.rs fn run() — the single-id claim/close path (branch = format!("work/{id}")).
  2. src/bin/bl-delivery.rs fn prime() — the prime.post re-materialization loop (bl-3d75), same literal.
  3. src/bin/bl-delivery.rs fn doctor() + src/delivery.rs worktree_path/binding_territory — the §16 audit (bl-0ec3) enumerates the same territory.
delivery::worktree_path already centralizes the PATH; the 'work/{id}' BRANCH string is the duplicated bit, and claimant is not yet threaded into either.

CHANGE. Introduce one helper (e.g. delivery::work_branch(id[, claimant]) alongside worktree_path, both taking the optional claimant key) and route all three call sites through it, BEFORE adding the claimant dimension. Then claimant-keying is a one-helper edit, not a 3-site hunt. Pure refactor first (no behavior change, keep 100% coverage); claimant-keying itself is a separate follow-on once §11's 'if ever needed' becomes needed.

RELATES bl-3d75 (prime loop), bl-0ec3 (doctor audit), bl-f813 (original claim/close derivation).
title: Converge work/<id> worktree-path derivation onto one helper before claimant-keying
